name: NACA 0012
description: A symmetric airfoil with 12% thickness. Commonly used on helicopter rotors, stabilizers, and high-speed applications. Produces zero lift at zero angle of attack.

name: NACA 2412
description: A classic general-purpose airfoil with 2% camber and 12% thickness. Widely used on light aircraft and general aviation. Offers good lift characteristics with moderate drag.

name: NACA 4412
description: A moderately cambered airfoil with 4% camber. Produces higher lift at low speeds, making it suitable for light aircraft and gliders. Has a higher zero-lift angle offset.

name: Clark Y
description: A classic flat-bottom airfoil with a slightly curved upper surface. Known for its high lift and gentle stall characteristics. Popular in early aircraft and model airplanes.

name: Selig S1223
camberDescription: High camber for low Reynolds number
description: A highly cambered airfoil designed for low Reynolds number applications like UAVs and gliders. Produces very high lift coefficients but also higher drag. Excellent for slow flight.

name: Eppler E205
description: A modern laminar flow airfoil with moderate camber. Designed for efficient cruise performance with low drag. Commonly used in sailplanes and high-efficiency aircraft.
